metadata.estimation_approach: Planning Poker consensus + complexity analysis + historical velocity

complexity_factors.HIGH: Algorithm complexity (DFS), external integrations (S3, Email), concurrent locking
complexity_factors.MEDIUM: Standard CRUD operations, state machines, validation logic
complexity_factors.LOW: UI components, simple filters, read-only features

1_point.examples: Add a single CSS property, rename a variable

5_points.examples: Component + API endpoint, simple UI with logic

8_points.examples: State machine, data validation, basic algorithm

13_points.examples: Complex algorithm (DAG), major integration, performance optimization

21_points.examples: Major refactoring, system redesign, complex feature set

stories.title: Create individual task
stories.user_story: As a team member, I want to create a task with title, description, and metadata, So that I can break down project work into manageable items
stories.complexity_rationale: Simple CRUD with Prisma transaction; requires basic form validation and API endpoint
stories.blockers: FOUNDATION - No blockers; blocks 9 other stories
stories.technical_notes: Use Prisma transaction for atomicity; validate title max 255 chars

stories.title: Implement priority levels with visual indicators
stories.user_story: As a team member viewing my task list, I want to see priority levels with color/icon indicators, So that I can focus on high-priority work first
stories.complexity_rationale: Simple enum + UI styling; CSS custom properties; filtering/sorting logic is straightforward
stories.blockers: INDEPENDENT - Can run in parallel with US-3.1.1
stories.technical_notes: Use CSS custom properties for DataDog dark mode compatibility

stories.title: Implement 5-status workflow with state machine
stories.user_story: As a task assignee, I want to move tasks through a defined workflow, So that project managers have visibility of progress
stories.complexity_rationale: State machine design requires careful transition logic; must prevent invalid transitions; audit trail needed
stories.blockers: CRITICAL PATH - Blocks US-3.4.2 (notifications) and US-3.2.1 (dependencies)

effort_breakdown.testing: 2 hours (state machine tests + race condition tests)

stories.technical_notes: Implement as enum-based validation; prevent skip transitions; audit trail required

stories.title: Assign task to team members
stories.user_story: As a project manager, I want to assign a task to one or more team members, So that team knows who is responsible for completion
stories.complexity_rationale: Multi-select dropdown requires user module integration; notifications (queue-based) needed; audit log tracking
stories.technical_notes: Use event-driven notifications (queue-based); integrate with User module

dependencies_ready: US-3.1.1 must complete before US-3.1.2 starts (day 5)

phase_1_sprint_2_backlog.sprint_capacity: 30 SP (10% velocity increase from team cohesion)

stories.title: Update task status and progress
stories.user_story: As a task assignee, I want to update task status and progress percentage, So that team visibility of task completion increases
stories.complexity_rationale: State machine validation (from US-3.4.1); optimistic locking required; notifications on change
stories.technical_notes: Optimize with Prisma transaction; implement optimistic locking via version timestamp

stories.title: Blocked status notifications
stories.user_story: As a project manager, I want to be notified when a task is marked Blocked, So that I can unblock dependencies quickly
stories.complexity_rationale: Simple event handler + notification queue; reuses notification infrastructure from US-3.1.2
stories.technical_notes: Use pub/sub pattern; integrate with DataDog alerts

stories.title: Define dependency relationships
stories.user_story: As a project manager, I want to define that Task B depends on Task A, So that team understands task sequencing
stories.complexity_rationale: DAG validation required; DFS cycle detection algorithm; visual task list changes; N+1 query risk on large graphs
stories.blockers: CRITICAL PATH - Blocks Phase 3 timeline calculation (US-3.2.3) and Gantt charts
stories.technical_notes: Use DFS for cycle detection; cache graph; set max depth = 10 to prevent pathological nesting
stories.algorithm_risk: DFS implementation complexity; recommend spike in Sprint 1 week 1

stories.title: Bulk task creation from CSV
stories.user_story: As a project manager, I want to import multiple tasks from CSV at once, So that I can set up project from templates or exports
stories.complexity_rationale: CSV parsing + validation; batch transaction; preview UI; rate limiting (100 tasks max)
stories.blockers: No downstream dependencies; can defer to Phase 2 if capacity-constrained
stories.technical_notes: Use streaming for large files; validate before transaction; implement rate limiting

phase_1_sprint_2_backlog.velocity_notes: Sprint 2 sees +10% velocity increase due to team cohesion; both US-3.2.1 and US-3.1.4 are complex but team is ready

stories.title: Log actual hours; store time tracking
stories.user_story: As a task assignee, I want to log hours spent on a task, So that team can track actual effort vs. estimates
stories.complexity_rationale: New data model (TimeLog); basic CRUD; permission checks; no complex algorithms

effort_breakdown.permissions: 1 hour (only assigned users + PM can view)

stories.technical_notes: Store as separate TimeLog entities; aggregate for dashboard in US-3.6.2

stories.title: Attach files; store in external storage
stories.user_story: As a task assignee, I want to attach relevant files to a task, So that all context is available without email
stories.complexity_rationale: S3 integration (cold start); file validation (MIME type, size); pre-signed URLs; rate limiting; error handling
stories.blockers: Requires S3/MinIO provisioning before Sprint 3 start

effort_breakdown.testing: 2 hours (S3 integration tests, file validation tests)

stories.technical_notes: Use pre-signed URLs for direct S3 upload; implement rate limiting (5 uploads/min); S3 must be provisioned in Sprint 1
stories.estimation_note: Original estimate of 10 SP is not Fibonacci; upgraded to 13 SP for S3 integration complexity

stories.title: Add comments to tasks; threaded discussion UI
stories.user_story: As a team member, I want to comment on tasks with support for nested replies, So that discussions stay within context
stories.complexity_rationale: Comment model + one-level nesting; markdown parsing (markdown-it); XSS prevention

effort_breakdown.frontend_ui: 2 hours (comment form, markdown preview, reply UI)

stories.technical_notes: Use markdown-it for safe HTML rendering; prevent XSS with sanitization library

stories.title: Create subtasks; manual progress tracking
stories.user_story: As a task assignee, I want to create subtasks for complex work, So that I can track granular progress
stories.complexity_rationale: Subtask model (parent-child); cascade delete logic; UI collapsible list; max 50 subtasks per task

effort_breakdown.data_model: 1 hour (Subtask schema with FK ON DELETE CASCADE)

stories.technical_notes: Use foreign key with ON DELETE CASCADE; implement subtask count check

phase_2_sprint_3_backlog.capacity_warning: ⚠️ Sprint 3 backlog exceeds 32 SP capacity by 5 SP. Recommend moving US-3.7.1 (13 SP) to Sprint 4 if team velocity doesn't sustain +10%
phase_2_sprint_3_backlog.velocity_notes: High parallelization in Sprint 3; 4 independent streams can run concurrently with different teams
phase_2_sprint_3_backlog.contingency_plan: If team can't sustain 37 SP, defer US-3.7.1 to Sprint 4 (reduces to 24 SP in Sprint 3, within capacity)

stories.title: Compare time logs vs. estimates; basic reporting
stories.user_story: As a project manager, I want to see actual hours vs. estimated hours, So that I can improve estimation accuracy
stories.complexity_rationale: Aggregate queries (Prisma groupBy); simple dashboard widget; CSV export (straightforward)
stories.technical_notes: Use Prisma groupBy for aggregations; no real-time time tracking

stories.title: File versioning (previous versions accessible)
stories.user_story: As a task assignee, I want to see previous versions of an attachment, So that I can revert or compare changes
stories.complexity_rationale: S3 versioning API; version history tracking; cleanup job for old versions; DB schema updates

effort_breakdown.cleanup_job: 1 hour (cron job to delete versions >30 days old)

stories.technical_notes: Use S3 object versioning; implement cleanup job (cron, runs nightly); max 5 versions per file

stories.title: Implement @mentions; send notifications
stories.user_story: As a commenter, I want to @mention team members, So that they are notified about discussions
stories.complexity_rationale: Mention parsing (regex); autocomplete dropdown; reuses notification queue from Phase 1
stories.technical_notes: Use regex for mention detection; debounce autocomplete; deduplicate mentions

stories.title: Auto-calculate completion % from subtasks
stories.user_story: As a project manager, I want task progress to auto-update when subtasks complete, So that parent task progress reflects child progress
stories.complexity_rationale: Simple percentage calculation; batch cron job (runs every 5 min); eventual consistency (within 1 min)

effort_breakdown.testing: 1 hour (percentage calculation tests + batch job tests)

stories.technical_notes: Implement as scheduled job (cron, runs every 5 min); eventual consistent (update within 1 min)

stories.title: Attach files; store in external storage
stories.user_story: As a task assignee, I want to attach relevant files to a task, So that all context is available without email
stories.complexity_rationale: S3 integration; file validation; pre-signed URLs; rate limiting (deferred from Sprint 3 if over capacity)
stories.blockers: Contingency story: move here from Sprint 3 if capacity-constrained
stories.technical_notes: Contingency story
stories.contingency_note: Move from Sprint 3 if sprint 3 velocity can't sustain 37 SP

phase_2_sprint_4_backlog.velocity_notes: Sprint 4 is lighter (23 SP) if Sprint 3 completes on time; can absorb US-3.7.1 contingency if needed
phase_2_sprint_4_backlog.contingency_plan: If Sprint 3 velocity drops below 32 SP, move US-3.7.1 (13 SP) to Sprint 4

stories.title: Validate circular dependencies; prevent invalid graphs
stories.user_story: As a project manager, I want validation to prevent circular task dependencies, So that task graphs remain valid DAGs
stories.complexity_rationale: Advanced cycle detection; likely using existing US-3.2.1 DFS as foundation; additional edge case handling

effort_breakdown.error_messages: 1 hour (clear feedback to user on cycle detection)

stories.technical_notes: Build on top of US-3.2.1 DFS algorithm; handle transitive dependency chains

stories.title: Calculate timeline impacts on dependent tasks
stories.user_story: As a project manager, I want to see how task delays impact dependent tasks, So that I can understand timeline cascade effects
stories.complexity_rationale: Complex cascade logic; event-driven updates; queue-based processing; must handle transitive dependency chains
stories.blockers: CRITICAL PATH - Blocks Gantt chart visualization and advanced reporting
stories.technical_notes: Event-driven cascade updates; queue-based processing; NOT real-time (eventual consistent within 5 min)
stories.algorithm_risk: Cascade recalculation is non-trivial; recommend spike in Phase 2

stories.title: File security (MIME validation, virus scanning)
stories.user_story: As a team member, I want uploaded files to be scanned for malware, So that task attachments are safe
stories.complexity_rationale: External virus scanning service integration (ClamAV or VirusTotal); async scanning workflow; quarantine/block logic
stories.blockers: No downstream dependencies; security hardening feature
stories.technical_notes: Phase 1 US-3.7.1 has basic MIME type validation; this adds advanced security

team_capacity_assumptions.dependency: Phase 2 completion and team velocity stability

phase_3_backlog.velocity_notes: Phase 3 assumes stable 32 SP/sprint velocity continuing from Phase 2
phase_3_backlog.estimation_note: Phase 3 stories are unsized in BRD; Agile Coach estimated based on complexity and Phase 2 patterns

velocity_progression.notes: HIGH PARALLELIZATION; 4 independent streams; may exceed capacity; contingency needed
Lighter sprint (dependencies from Sprint 3); can absorb contingency
Phase 3; estimated based on Phase 2 patterns; assumes stable 32 SP/sprint

estimates_verified_by: Effort-to-points mapping (5-6 hours = 5 points, 8 hours = 8 points, etc.)
Risk factors (complexity, external dependencies, algorithm difficulty)

confidence_levels.phase_2: MEDIUM (70% confidence; external service integrations add uncertainty)
confidence_levels.phase_3: MEDIUM (65% confidence; unsized stories estimated from Phase 2 patterns)

velocity_adjustment_trigger.condition: If Sprint 1-2 actuals vary >15% from plan
velocity_adjustment_trigger.action: Re-estimate Phase 2 stories; adjust Sprint 3 capacity

planning_recommendations: Sprint 1-2 (Phase 1): Conservative planning with velocity ramp-up; allocate best developers to US-3.1.1 (foundation)
Sprint 3: HIGH PARALLELIZATION with contingency plan; if Sprint 1-2 velocity < 28 SP average, defer US-3.7.1 to Sprint 4
Sprint 4: Lighter sprint (23 SP) allows buffer for contingencies or process improvements
Phase 3: Estimate based on actual Phase 2 velocity; consider velocity stability post-Phase 2
risk_management: HIGH RISK: US-3.2.1 (DAG algorithm) and US-3.2.3 (cascade logic) - Schedule spikes in Sprint 1 and Phase 2 respectively
HIGH RISK: US-3.7.1 (S3 integration) - Provision MinIO in Sprint 1; test integration early
HIGH RISK: SQLite write contention - Load test by Sprint 1 week 3; have PostgreSQL migration contingency ready
MEDIUM RISK: Sprint 3 over-capacity - Build contingency buffer; be ready to defer US-3.7.1 if needed
velocity_management: Assume +10% velocity growth from Sprint 1 to Sprint 2 (team cohesion)
Plan for stable velocity in Phase 2 sprints
Monitor actual vs. planned velocity; adjust Phase 3 estimates if variance > 15%
Plan retrospectives after Sprint 1 and Sprint 2 to refine estimation accuracy
resource_allocation: Sprint 1: 4 developers (Backend Lead on US-3.1.1, Frontend Lead on US-3.3.1, Backend on US-3.4.1 and US-3.1.2)
Sprint 2: Same team; add DevOps for S3/MinIO setup
Sprint 3-4: Expand to 5-6 developers for 4 parallel streams; pair programming on high-risk stories
Phase 3: Reduce to 3-4 developers; focus on complex algorithm stories (US-3.2.3)
quality_assurance: Target >80% Jest coverage on all stories
Definition of Done: code review, tests, manual QA, DataDog integration
Performance testing: US-3.2.1 (1000 task graphs), US-3.7.1 (100MB uploads), US-3.1.4 (1000 bulk imports)
Security audit: before Phase 1 go-live for optimistic locking, user permissions, XSS prevention
